analysis_context.objective_metric: negative log2 differential probability of a single differential characteristic
analysis_context.validation_anchor_ref: eprint-2016-407 Table 2 Speck32 differential-characteristic summary row
analysis_context.notes: This IR is task-scoped to the 9-round paper checkpoint rather than to the full 22-round Speck-32/64 primitive.

index_domains.purpose: The 9 encryption rounds inside the current analysis scope.
State boundaries before round 0 and after each modeled round.
Key-schedule updates needed to derive round keys K1 through K8 from the initial K0.
The three auxiliary Speck key-schedule slots that form the rolling queue.
Bit positions of a 16-bit Speck word.

state_families.summary: The left 16-bit encryption branch at the input boundary of each modeled round.
The right 16-bit encryption branch at the input boundary of each modeled round.
The left branch after the round-local right rotation by 7.
The intermediate left branch after modular addition and before xor with the round key.
The 16-bit round key consumed by each modeled encryption round.
The right branch after the round-local left rotation by 2.
The three auxiliary Speck schedule words before each modeled key update.
The currently selected auxiliary schedule word after the right rotation by 7.
The selected schedule word after modular addition with the current round key.
The selected schedule word after xor with the round index constant.
The current round key after the key-schedule left rotation by 2.
The round index constant injected during each modeled Speck key update.

operator_templates.name: Encryption rotate left branch right
operator_templates.summary: Rotate the left encryption branch right by 7 in each modeled round.

operator_templates.name: Encryption modular add branches

refinement_requirements.required_for: exact differential-weight propagation through the Speck round addition
refinement_requirements.summary: Later semantic refinement must attach xor-differential modular-add semantics to the encryption add_mod template.

operator_templates.summary: Add the rotated left branch and the right branch modulo 2^16 in each modeled round.

operator_templates.name: Encryption xor round key
operator_templates.summary: Xor the current round key into the modular-add result to obtain the updated left branch carried to the next round boundary.

operator_templates.name: Encryption rotate right branch left
operator_templates.summary: Rotate the right encryption branch left by 2 in each modeled round.

operator_templates.name: Encryption xor branches
operator_templates.summary: Xor the rotated right branch with the updated left branch to obtain the next right-boundary value.

operator_templates.name: Key schedule rotate selected auxiliary word
operator_templates.summary: Select the active auxiliary schedule slot by i mod 3 and rotate that word right by 7.

operator_templates.name: Key schedule modular add with round key

refinement_requirements.required_for: exact differential propagation through the Speck key-schedule addition
refinement_requirements.summary: Later semantic refinement must attach xor-differential modular-add semantics to the key-schedule add_mod template.

operator_templates.summary: Add the rotated auxiliary schedule word and the current round key modulo 2^16.

operator_templates.name: Key schedule xor round index
operator_templates.summary: Xor the round index constant into the selected auxiliary schedule word.

operator_templates.name: Key schedule rotate round key
operator_templates.summary: Rotate the current round key left by 2 before generating the next round key.

operator_templates.name: Key schedule generate next round key
operator_templates.summary: Commit the updated auxiliary schedule word back to the active queue slot and xor it with the rotated round key to produce the next round key.

indexing_relations.active_selector: selected auxiliary schedule word is slot i mod 3
indexing_relations.selection_rule: At key update i, the active auxiliary schedule word is the slot schedule_l_words_pre_update[i mod 3].
indexing_relations.advancement_rule: After key update i, the updated auxiliary word is written back to that same slot, the xor result becomes round_key for encryption round i+1, and the active slot advances to (i+1) mod 3.
indexing_relations.summary: Speck-32/64 uses one active round key together with a cyclic queue of three auxiliary schedule words.

dataflow_edges.summary: The left branch boundary value feeds the encryption right-rotation step.
The rotation step produces the rotated left branch.
The rotated left branch is one input of the encryption modular-add template.
The current right branch is the second input of the encryption modular-add template.
The modular-add template produces the intermediate left-branch sum.
The modular-add sum is one input of the round-key xor template.
The current round key is the second input of the round-key xor template.
The xor-with-round-key step yields the updated left branch carried to the next round boundary.
The right branch boundary value feeds the encryption left-rotation step.
The rotation step produces the rotated right branch.
The rotated right branch is one input of the final xor template.
The updated left branch from enc_xor_round_key is reused in the same logical round as the second input of the final xor template.
The final xor step yields the updated right branch carried to the next round boundary.
The rolling auxiliary schedule-word array feeds the template that selects and rotates slot i mod 3.
The selected auxiliary schedule word is produced after the right rotation by 7.
The rotated auxiliary schedule word is one input of the key-schedule modular addition.
The current round key is the second input of the key-schedule modular addition.
The key-schedule modular addition produces the updated auxiliary schedule word before constant injection.
The updated auxiliary schedule word feeds the round-index xor template.
The key-schedule round index constant is injected by xor.
The round-index xor produces the auxiliary schedule word after constant injection.
The current round key feeds the key-schedule left-rotation template.
The key-schedule left rotation produces the rotated current round key.
The rotated round key is one input of the final key-generation xor template.
The updated auxiliary schedule word after constant injection is the second input of the final key-generation xor template.
The final key-generation xor yields the next round key, which is consumed by encryption round i+1.
The final key-generation step also commits the updated auxiliary schedule word back into the active queue slot.

observation_points.summary: The encryption modular-add templates across rounds 0 through 8 form the primary objective surface for the reported trail-weight checkpoint.
The 9-round task is interpreted at the boundary after round 8 together with the companion right-branch family and compared against the paper's reported Table 2 checkpoint.

semantic_layers_not_yet_attached: No concrete xor-differential modular-add rule pack is attached to the encryption or key-schedule add_mod templates yet.
No backend-specific variable families or inequalities are attached yet.
The rolling-queue indexing relation is declared, but it is not yet expanded into fully instantiated per-round slot objects.
blocking_gaps: Dataflow edges remain summary-heavy for same-round boundary reuse between the updated left and right branches.
The current IR does not yet encode an executable unrolling procedure from rolling-queue relations to per-round key-schedule slot instances.
provenance_and_gaps.notes: This IR is intentionally structural and task-scoped. It is explicit enough for later semantic refinement, but still earlier than backend emission.
